summary: ldpbootstrap-jquery ships an HTA-style hidden-PowerShell dropper as its only API
details: The package's sole library function `LdpBootstrap.run(config)` (in dist/bootstrap.js) fetches an opaque PowerShell payload, XOR-decrypts it with a key retrieved at runtime from a caller-supplied `payloadKeyUrl`, writes the resulting.ps1 to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Landpage\<ps1FileName>`, and launches `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -WindowStyle Hidden -File <path>` via WScript.Shell. The decryptor itself is shipped alongside the encrypted payload (`dist/ps1-stub.enc.hex`, ~14 KB hex blob) implementing repeating-key XOR (`String.fromCharCode(a[i]^t[i%t.length])`). The combination — opaque encrypted payload, runtime key fetch, hidden PowerShell window, RemoteSigned execution policy — is a canonical Windows dropper engine designed to defeat static review and AV. The package is published publicly on npm (and aliased through jsdelivr) and self-describes as 'HTA install bootstrap for Landpage (CDN distribution)' / 'canal de distribuição do runtime e do PS1 stub ofuscado'. The `-jquery` suffix on the package name is namespace camouflage. Any consumer that imports this package and invokes its only exported method becomes a remote-PowerShell execution target with the actual code never visible to the consumer or to scanners. License is UNLICENSED.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
